summary: A subcontract for aluminium windows and curtain walling to the Harbour Crest Residences tower. Commercial risk is concentrated in the payment, retention and delay-damages terms, an extended defects liability period, and a fitness-for-purpose warranty that would sit outside professional-indemnity cover.
scope_responsibilities: Supply, fabrication and installation of aluminium windows and unitised curtain walling
Provision of branded site hoarding and signage at the perimeter
obligations: Air and water penetration testing of curtain wall mock-ups
Structural glazing certification prior to installation
client_assumptions: Payment to the subcontractor is conditional on the Contractor being paid by the Employer
Retention is released only at Final Certificate, not at Practical Completion
interfaces: Structural frame tolerances at slab edges
Waterproofing membrane lap with the facade at parapets
clarifications: Confirm whether the fitness-for-purpose warranty is intended to override manufacturer warranties
Confirm the aggregate cap (if any) on liquidated damages
